title: Able Assess vs Jamar Dynamometer
description: Side-by-side comparison of Able Assess and the Jamar hand dynamometer. Digital data capture, normative comparison, and workflow integration vs analogue measurement.

seo.title: Able Assess vs Jamar Dynamometer | Able Care
seo.description: Compare Able Assess with the Jamar hand dynamometer. Digital grip strength measurement with automatic data capture, normative scoring and workflow integration.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Close-up of GripAble digital grip strength measurement
blocks.headline: Able Assess vs Jamar dynamometer.
blocks.subtitle: Digital grip strength measurement with automatic data capture, normative comparison and full workflow integration. Everything analogue cannot do.

blocks.heading: Head-to-head comparison
blocks.tldr: The Jamar dynamometer is the traditional analogue grip strength tool. It captures a single reading with no digital record, no normative comparison, and no route into a care plan. Able Assess captures grip strength digitally alongside three additional functional metrics, scores every result against age- and sex-matched normative data, and feeds risk banding straight into the clinical workflow.

values: Fully digital, sensor-based grip strength via the GripAble device
Analogue hydraulic gauge, read visually by the operator
Automatic digital capture with timestamped records and longitudinal trending
Manual reading, recorded by hand on paper or spreadsheet
Automatic scoring against age- and sex-matched normative data
Clinician must look up normative tables manually
Four metrics in one workflow: grip strength, sit-to-stand, gait speed, timed up-and-go
Risk banding routed to care team, EHR and OASIS integration-ready
No digital workflow, no integration capability
One standardized protocol across every site and every staff member
Reading accuracy varies by operator technique and gauge calibration
Any staff member, no clinical certification required
Typically requires trained clinical staff for correct positioning and reading
Roll out digitally to every branch with minimal training
One device per site, no centralized data, no trend reporting

blocks.heading: Why digital grip strength measurement matters
blocks.pulledStat: Grip strength predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure.

citations.title: Grip strength is a validated marker for frailty, sarcopenia and functional decline in older adults
Multifactorial screening outperforms single-test approaches in predicting falls
NICE 2025 guidelines recommend standardized, objective falls risk screening

blocks.bodyText: A 20-minute demo comparing analogue and digital grip measurement, side by side.
